# qrstudio - SEO + GEO Remediation Playbook

**Target**: https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai
**Backing service**: qr-studio/ssl-redirect → app.qr (real app in qr-studio-api/qr-studio-web)
**Source repo (best guess)**: intelli-verse-x/qr-studio-web + qr-studio-api
**Audit reference**: `reports/qrstudio.md`
**Generated**: 2026-05-17T18:35:00Z
**Generated by**: gastown audits/crew/auditor (firecrawl + claude)

---

## 1. Executive summary

QRStudio is a **client-side SPA with near-zero server-rendered content**—the most challenging SEO/GEO scenario. The HTML shell is empty (`<div id="root">`), robots.txt and sitemap.xml return SPA HTML instead of proper files (routing misconfiguration), and there's no OG tags, structured data, or security headers. Search engines can execute JavaScript but with delays; AI systems may not index JS-only content at all. The headline fix is twofold: (1) **immediate**: fix robots/sitemap routing, enhance HTML shell metadata; (2) **long-term**: migrate to SSR/SSG framework (Next.js, Remix, SvelteKit). Without SSR, this site will always underperform for SEO/GEO. Projected impact: 2/10 → 5/10 LLM-friendliness with quick fixes; 2/10 → 8/10 with SSR migration.

## 2. Current state recap

From `reports/qrstudio.md`:

**PASS/OK**:
- ✓ **title tag** ([report#L10](reports/qrstudio.md#L10)): "QR Studio" (could be more descriptive)
- ✓ **meta description** ([report#L11](reports/qrstudio.md#L11)): 90 chars (could expand to 155)
- ✓ **lang attribute** ([report#L19](reports/qrstudio.md#L19)): `<html lang="en">`

**FAIL/WARN**:
- ✗ **canonical** ([report#L12](reports/qrstudio.md#L12)): Missing (WARN)
- ✗ **robots.txt** ([report#L13](reports/qrstudio.md#L13)): Returns SPA HTML (FAIL)
- ✗ **sitemap.xml** ([report#L14](reports/qrstudio.md#L14)): Returns SPA HTML (FAIL)
- ✗ **HSTS** ([report#L15](reports/qrstudio.md#L15)): Missing security header (FAIL)
- ✗ **Open Graph** ([report#L16](reports/qrstudio.md#L16)): Missing og:* tags (FAIL)
- ✗ **Twitter Card** ([report#L17](reports/qrstudio.md#L17)): Missing twitter:* tags (FAIL)
- ✗ **JSON-LD schema** ([report#L18](reports/qrstudio.md#L18)): No structured data (WARN)
- ✗ **h1 count** ([report#L18](reports/qrstudio.md#L18)): Cannot assess, CSR-only (FAIL)
- ✗ **CSP** ([report#L20](reports/qrstudio.md#L20)): Missing Content-Security-Policy (WARN)
- ✗ **Server-side rendering** ([report#L22](reports/qrstudio.md#L22)): CSR-only, search engines see empty shell (CRITICAL)

**GEO findings** ([report#L24-L45](reports/qrstudio.md#L24-L45)): AI-answerability FAIL (2/10). Empty `<div id="root">`, all content via JavaScript. AI systems that don't execute JS see blank page.

## 3. 2026 best-practice baseline

Same foundational research as prior remediations (see `remediation/ai-host.md` section 3).

**SPA SEO-specific guidance**:

**Source**: [Google Search Central - JavaScript SEO](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/javascript/javascript-seo-basics) (2026)

> "Googlebot can render and understand JavaScript-based websites, but there are some caveats... For the best SEO results, we recommend server-side rendering or pre-rendering."

**2026 status**: Google can execute JS but with delays (up to 7 days for initial render). Other search engines (Bing, DuckDuckGo) have weaker JS support. AI crawlers (GPTBot, Claude-Web) may not execute JS at all or have strict execution limits.

**Best practice**: Use SSR (Next.js, Remix, SvelteKit, Nuxt) or SSG for at least key pages (homepage, pricing, features).

## 4. Concrete remediation (prioritized)

### Phase 1: Immediate fixes (no code migration required)

### 1. Fix robots.txt and sitemap.xml routing

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Configure server to serve static files BEFORE SPA fallback
- **Why**: Fixes robots/sitemap returning HTML ([report#L13-L14](reports/qrstudio.md#L13-L14)), unblocks crawler indexing
- **Where**: Nginx config (likely in k8s ConfigMap or ingress annotations)
- **How** (Nginx):

```nginx
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai;
    
    root /var/www/qrstudio;
    
    # Serve static SEO files directly (before SPA fallback)
    location = /robots.txt {
        try_files /robots.txt =404;
    }
    
    location = /sitemap.xml {
        try_files /sitemap.xml =404;
    }
    
    location = /llms.txt {
        try_files /llms.txt =404;
    }
    
    # SPA fallback for all other routes
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
    }
}
```

**Or** if using k8s Ingress with nginx-ingress-controller, add annotation:

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: qrstudio-ingress
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/configuration-snippet: |
      location = /robots.txt { try_files /robots.txt =404; }
      location = /sitemap.xml { try_files /sitemap.xml =404; }
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt | head -5
# Expected: "User-agent: *" (not HTML)

curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | head -5
# Expected: "<?xml version" (not HTML)
```

### 2. Create robots.txt

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 15 minutes
- **What**: Add static robots.txt to public directory
- **Where**: `qr-studio-web/public/robots.txt` (new file)
- **How**:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Allow: /

User-agent: Claude-Web
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
```

- **Verify**: See step 1 verification

### 3. Create sitemap.xml

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Add static sitemap listing key routes
- **Where**: `qr-studio-web/public/sitemap.xml` (new file)
- **How**:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>weekly</changefreq>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
  <!-- Add other public routes if they exist: /pricing, /features, /docs, etc. -->
</urlset>
```

**Note**: If the SPA has client-side routes (e.g., `/pricing`, `/features`), add them to sitemap. But they must be **public** (not auth-required) to be crawlable.

- **Verify**: See step 1 verification

### 4. Enhance index.html metadata

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 1 hour
- **What**: Add canonical, OG tags, Twitter Card, JSON-LD to HTML shell
- **Why**: Fixes missing metadata ([report#L12,L15-L18](reports/qrstudio.md#L12)), provides fallback for crawlers that don't execute JS
- **Where**: `qr-studio-web/index.html` or `public/index.html`
- **How**: Add to `<head>` section:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    
    <!-- Existing title and description (enhance these) -->
    <title>QR Studio | Dynamic QR Code Generator & Analytics Platform</title>
    <meta name="description" content="Design dynamic and static QR codes, build custom landing pages, and track scan analytics. Professional QR code management platform for businesses and creators. Free to start.">
    
    <!-- Canonical URL -->
    <link rel="canonical" href="https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <!-- Open Graph -->
    <meta property="og:title" content="QR Studio | Dynamic QR Code Generator">
    <meta property="og:description" content="Design, track, and manage dynamic QR codes with custom landing pages. Professional QR code analytics platform.">
    <meta property="og:type" content="website">
    <meta property="og:url" content="https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    <meta property="og:site_name" content="QR Studio">
    <!-- Add og:image once you have a suitable image -->
    <!-- <meta property="og:image" content="https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/og-image.png"> -->
    
    <!-- Twitter Card -->
    <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
    <meta name="twitter:title" content="QR Studio | Dynamic QR Code Generator">
    <meta name="twitter:description" content="Design, track, and manage dynamic QR codes with custom landing pages.">
    <!-- <meta name="twitter:image" content="https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/twitter-card.png"> -->
    
    <!-- JSON-LD Structured Data -->
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
      "@context": "https://schema.org",
      "@type": "WebApplication",
      "name": "QR Studio",
      "url": "https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai",
      "description": "Design dynamic and static QR codes, build custom landing pages, and track scan analytics.",
      "applicationCategory": "DesignApplication",
      "offers": {
        "@type": "Offer",
        "price": "0",
        "priceCurrency": "USD"
      },
      "featureList": [
        "Dynamic QR code generation",
        "Static QR code generation",
        "Custom landing page builder",
        "Scan analytics and tracking",
        "QR code design customization"
      ]
    }
    </script>
    
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
      "@context": "https://schema.org",
      "@type": "Organization",
      "name": "QR Studio",
      "url": "https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai",
      "logo": "https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/logo.png"
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="root"></div>
    <!-- React/Vue/etc app mounts here -->
</body>
</html>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E 'rel="canonical"|og:|twitter:|application/ld\+json'
# Expected: matches for all metadata types
```

### 5. Add security headers

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Configure HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options
- **Why**: Fixes missing security headers ([report#L15,L20](reports/qrstudio.md#L15)), improves SEO ranking
- **Where**: Nginx config
- **How**:

```nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "no-referrer-when-downgrade" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';" always;
```

**Note**: CSP for SPAs often requires `'unsafe-inline'` and `'unsafe-eval'` for dev builds. Tighten in production if possible.

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sSI https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -iE 'strict-transport|x-content-type|x-frame'
# Expected: 3 matches
```

### 6. Add llms.txt

- **Priority**: P2 — estimated 1 hour
- **What**: Create `/llms.txt` for GEO
- **Where**: `qr-studio-web/public/llms.txt` (new file)
- **How**:

```markdown
# QR Studio

> QR Studio is a dynamic and static QR code generator with custom landing page builder and scan analytics tracking. Professional QR code management platform for businesses and creators.

Key features:

- **Dynamic QR codes**: Update destination URL without reprinting the QR code
- **Static QR codes**: Generate permanent QR codes for fixed content
- **Landing page builder**: Create custom landing pages for QR code destinations
- **Scan analytics**: Track scans by location, device, time, and user demographics
- **Design customization**: Customize QR code colors, logos, and styles

Use cases:

- Marketing campaigns (track ROI, A/B test landing pages)
- Event management (registration, check-in, feedback)
- Product packaging (serial numbers, warranty info, manuals)
- Business cards (contact info, portfolio links)
- Restaurant menus (contactless ordering)

## Documentation

- [Homepage](https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai): Platform overview
- [API Documentation](https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/docs): Developer API reference (if exists)

## Related Services

- [IntelliVerse X Platform](https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai): User account management
- [IntelliVerse X Home](https://intelli-verse-x.ai): Ecosystem overview
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt | head -20
# Expected: "# QR Studio"
```

---

### Phase 2: Long-term migration (recommended for 8/10+ SEO/GEO)

### 7. Migrate to SSR/SSG framework

- **Priority**: P1 (HIGH) — estimated 2-4 weeks (full migration)
- **What**: Migrate SPA to Next.js (React), Remix (React), SvelteKit (Svelte), or Nuxt (Vue)
- **Why**: CSR-only SPAs will always underperform for SEO/GEO ([report#L22](reports/qrstudio.md#L22)). SSR provides crawlable HTML.
- **Where**: New repo or branch for SSR version
- **How** (Next.js example):

**Option A: Incremental adoption** (keep existing SPA, add Next.js for marketing pages):
1. Create new Next.js app for `/` (homepage), `/pricing`, `/features`
2. Keep existing React SPA for `/app/*` (authenticated dashboard)
3. Nginx routes:
   - `/` → Next.js (SSR landing page)
   - `/app/*` → Existing React SPA

**Option B: Full migration** (rewrite SPA as Next.js App Router):
1. Create Next.js 14+ App Router project
2. Migrate components from React SPA to Next.js
3. Use `'use client'` for interactive components
4. Pre-render homepage, pricing, features as SSG
5. Keep dashboard as CSR (`'use client'` pages)

**Migration checklist**:
- [ ] Homepage with server-rendered hero, features, pricing
- [ ] Metadata via `export const metadata = {...}` in layouts
- [ ] Automatic sitemap generation (`next-sitemap` package)
- [ ] FAQ section with FAQPage schema
- [ ] Blog or docs section for content marketing (optional)

**Frameworks comparison** (2026):
- **Next.js**: Best React ecosystem, great SEO tools, Vercel hosting
- **Remix**: React with nested routes, excellent data loading
- **SvelteKit**: Faster bundle size, simpler syntax, growing ecosystem
- **Nuxt**: Best Vue ecosystem, strong SSR performance

- **Verify** (post-migration):
```bash
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -c '<h1>'
# Expected: 1 (visible h1 in server HTML, not empty div)

curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | wc -l
# Expected: > 200 lines (full HTML content, not 50-line shell)
```

## 5. Stack-aware notes

Based on the audit (`<div id="root">`), this is likely:
- **React SPA** (Create React App, Vite + React)
- **Vue SPA** (Vite + Vue, Vue CLI)
- **Svelte SPA** (Vite + Svelte)

**Architecture implications**:

1. **Current setup**: Build generates static files → nginx serves `index.html` for all routes (SPA fallback)

2. **Problem**: Nginx `try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html` catches `/robots.txt` and `/sitemap.xml`, serving `index.html` instead. Fix by adding explicit `location =` blocks (see remediation #1).

3. **Deployment**: Likely Docker image with nginx serving `/dist` or `/build` output. Verify:
   ```bash
   kubectl logs -n qr-studio deployment/qr-studio-web | grep nginx
   ```

4. **SSR migration impact**: Moving to Next.js/Remix/SvelteKit requires:
   - Node.js server (not just static nginx)
   - Different Docker image (node:alpine base, not nginx)
   - Different k8s deployment (app server on port 3000, not static files)
   - Environment variables for API endpoints

5. **Hybrid approach** (recommended if full migration is too disruptive):
   - Keep existing SPA for `/app/*` (auth-required dashboard)
   - Add Next.js SSR for `/` (public landing page)
   - Nginx routes public URLs to Next.js, authenticated URLs to SPA

## 6. Verification plan

After Phase 1 fixes:

```bash
# 1. Robots.txt and sitemap
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt | head -3
# Expected: "User-agent: *" (not HTML)

curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | grep '<?xml'
# Expected: match

# 2. Metadata in HTML shell
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E 'rel="canonical"|og:title|twitter:card|application/ld\+json'
# Expected: 4+ matches

# 3. Security headers
curl -sSI https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -i 'strict-transport'
# Expected: Strict-Transport-Security header

# 4. llms.txt
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt
# Expected: QR Studio content

# 5. GEO check (limited improvement without SSR)
firecrawl scrape https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-main-content -o /tmp/qrstudio-post.md
wc -l /tmp/qrstudio-post.md
# Expected: ~20-30 lines (meta description only, no rendered content yet)

# 6. Lighthouse audit
lighthouse https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-categories=seo --output=json
# Target: SEO 60-70 (up from ~30 before fixes, but capped without SSR)
```

After Phase 2 (SSR migration):

```bash
# 1. Server-rendered content
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep '<h1>'
# Expected: visible h1 tag in HTML (not empty div)

# 2. Content length
curl -sS https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai | wc -l
# Expected: > 300 lines (full page content)

# 3. GEO check (major improvement)
firecrawl scrape https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-main-content
# Expected: > 100 lines of content (features, pricing, FAQ, etc.)

# 4. Lighthouse audit
lighthouse https://qrstudio.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-categories=seo --output=json
# Target: SEO 90+ (near-perfect score with SSR)
```

## 7. Open questions / blockers

1. **Is this a Vite, CRA, or custom SPA?** — Need to inspect `package.json` to understand build tooling. This affects SSR migration path.

2. **Where are robots.txt/sitemap.xml currently?** — Verify they exist in repo (e.g., `public/` directory). If missing, remediation #2-3 create them. If present but not served, remediation #1 (nginx routing) fixes it.

3. **What routes exist in the SPA?** — Need list of client-side routes (e.g., `/pricing`, `/features`, `/docs`, `/app/dashboard`) to:
   - Add public routes to sitemap
   - Exclude auth routes from robots.txt
   - Plan SSR migration scope

4. **Is there a backend API?** — Repo guess mentions `qr-studio-api` + `qr-studio-web`. If separate API exists:
   - SSR migration can use same API
   - Verify API base URL environment variable
   - Ensure SSR server can access API (k8s networking)

5. **Who manages infrastructure?** — SSR migration requires:
   - New Docker image (node, not nginx)
   - Updated k8s deployment manifest
   - Potentially new Ingress rules
   - DevOps/platform team involvement

6. **What's the traffic split between public vs. auth pages?** — If 90% of traffic is to `/app/*` (dashboard), SSR migration ROI is lower. If 50%+ is public landing pages, SSR is critical.

7. **SSR framework preference?** — Next.js is most popular, but if team prefers Vue (Nuxt) or Svelte (SvelteKit), those work too. Check team expertise.

## 8. Estimated impact

### Phase 1: Immediate fixes (no SSR)

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 2/10 — CSR-only, robots/sitemap broken, no metadata
- **After**: 5/10 — metadata in HTML shell, proper robots/sitemap, security headers
- **Improvement**: Limited. Google can still crawl (with JS execution delay), but content is thin.
- **Timeline**: Fixes deploy in 1-2 days, indexing improves within 1 week

**GEO**:
- **Before**: 2/10 — AI sees empty HTML
- **After**: 4/10 — AI sees metadata (title, description, JSON-LD) but no rendered content
- **Improvement**: Marginal. LLMs can answer "what is QR Studio?" from description, but can't extract features, pricing, or detailed info.

**Traffic**:
- **Before**: Organic traffic limited by poor SEO
- **After**: +10-20% lift (mostly from fixed robots/sitemap, better metadata)

### Phase 2: SSR migration (long-term)

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 5/10 (post Phase 1)
- **After**: 9/10 — full server-rendered content, automatic sitemaps, FAQ schema
- **Improvement**: 50-100% organic traffic lift within 3-6 months
- **Timeline**: Migration takes 2-4 weeks, full SEO impact visible in 8-12 weeks

**GEO**:
- **Before**: 4/10 (post Phase 1)
- **After**: 8/10 — LLMs can extract features, pricing, use cases from rendered HTML
- **Improvement**: AI assistants can confidently answer:
  - "What features does QR Studio have?" → Lists dynamic/static codes, landing pages, analytics
  - "How much does QR Studio cost?" → Extracts pricing tiers (if page exists)
  - "What are QR codes used for?" → Explains use cases from content
- **Timeline**: LLM crawler re-index in 4-8 weeks, GEO impact visible in 2-3 months

**Traffic**:
- **Before** (Phase 1): ~100 organic visitors/month (estimate for new SaaS tool)
- **After** (Phase 2): ~200-400 organic visitors/month (+100-300% lift)
- **Long-term** (6+ months post-SSR + content): 500-1,000+ visitors/month

**Business impact**:
- SSR migration enables content marketing (blog, guides, templates)
- Better SEO → more free trial signups → higher revenue
- GEO improvement → AI assistants recommend QR Studio for "QR code generator" queries

**Recommendation**: Prioritize Phase 1 (quick wins, 1-2 days effort) immediately. Schedule Phase 2 (SSR migration) within next 1-2 quarters—it's essential for long-term SEO/GEO success.
